title: Compound Interest: Kenshi's dream is to buy a home, so he decides...
summary: Calculate the effective annual rate or convert between APR and EAR under different compounding assumptions.
prompt:
Kenshi's dream is to buy a home, so he decides to start a "real estate" fund. Note
that Kenshi deposited $38,937 into an investing account that pays 7.3488%
compounded 12 times per year. How large will Kenshi's "real estate" fund be in 15
years?
Select the closest
answer$128,829.29$122,694.56$116,851.96$113,930.66$111,082.40
solution:
Step 1 Highlight Relevant Information
Kenshi's dream is to buy a home, so he decides to start a "real estate" fund. Note
that Kenshi deposited $38,937 into an investing account that pays 7.3488%
compounded 12 times per year. How large will Kenshi's "real estate" fund be in
15 years? Also, will he have enough money to purchase a $252,185 home at that
time?
A few things to note:
The first highlighted bit (i.e., $38,937 represents "PV0" since it's the money
deposited by Kenshi as of today
The second highlighted bit (i.e., 7.3488% represents "QR"; therefore, this figure
must be compounded 12 times per year to earn Kenshi's Effective Annual Rate
EAR
The third highlighted bit (i.e., 15 years) represents the number of years Kenshi's
deposit will compound interest; therefore, this will be "N" when we compute for
the future value of his deposit
Calculator studio solutions 21
The fourth highlighted bit (i.e., a $252,185 home) will serve as a benchmark to
verify whether Kenshi has enough money to buy his dream home
The formula to set Quoted Rates to Effective Annual Rates can be written as
follows
EAR1QRm)m−1EAR \left 1\frac{QR}{m} \right )^{m}-1EAR1+mQR)m−1
QR 7.3488%
m: 12
The formula to solve for the future value of Kenshi's deposit can be written as
follows
FVn=PV0(1+k)nFV_{n}=PV_{0(1+k)^{n}FVn=PV0(1+k)n
PV0 $38,937
N 15
K EAR unknown for now!
FV15 ?
Step 2 Visualizing Quoted Rates to Effective Rates
Although you aren't requested to do this on an exam, it may be relevant to
visualize what a Quoted Rate and it's number of compounding periods actually
represents.
Timeline for Kenshi's Account
A few things to note:
Notice how QR was divided throughout the timeline (i.e., 7.3488% divided by 12
Notice how this account will compound 0.6% every 1 period(s) (i.e., every 1
months)
Step 3 Solving for Kenshi's Effective Annual Rate
The formula to set Quoted Rates to Effective Annual Rates can be written as
follows
EAR1QRm)m−1EAR \left 1\frac{QR}{m} \right )^{m}-1EAR1+mQR)m−1
QR 7.3488%
m: 12
Calculator studio solutions 22
Such that EAR 7.6015%
Step 4 Compounding Interest on Kenshi's Deposit
Timeline
The formula to solve for the future value of Kenshi's deposit can be written as
follows
FVn=PV0(1+k)nFV_{n}=PV_{0(1+k)^{n}FVn=PV0(1+k)n
PV0 $38,937
N 15
K EAR 7.6015%
Such that FV15 $116,851.96
